title: Diplomacy and international relations
explanation:
Diplomacy and international relations

  • treaty, sanction, envoy, sovereignty, alliance
  • negotiation, ceasefire, summit, ratification, embargo
  • verbs: mediate, deploy, recognise, escalate

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The two states signed a peace ____ after months of talks. | *treaty | ticket | recipe
The ambassador acted as an ____ during the crisis. | *envoy | enemy | editor
Economic ____ were imposed to pressure the regime. | *sanctions | salaries | slogans
National ____ was a key issue in the debate. | *sovereignty | solidarity | software

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Leaders met at an emergency ____ in Geneva. | summit
The UN attempted to ____ between the warring parties. | mediate
Parliament delayed ____ of the agreement. | ratification
Troops were ____ to the border region. | deployed
An ____ on oil exports increased tensions. | embargo

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Both sides agreed to a temporary ____.  | *ceasefire | campaign | concert
The military ____ strengthened regional security. | *alliance | audience | article
Analysts fear the conflict could ____ further. | *escalate | educate | estimate
Many countries refused to ____ the new government. | *recognise | recycle | recover
